name: Slow and Steady — 20 min
description: Everything at 50-60 BPM. A low-effort session focused on relaxation and connection, good for tired days or evenings.

items.label: Long tones at 50 BPM (5 min)
items.instruction: One note per two beats. Full length. Focus on tone, breath, and physical release. Every note should feel effortless.

items.label: One scale, ascending + descending, at 60 BPM (5 min)
items.instruction: Pick any scale. One note per beat. Full range. Repeat until 5 minutes are up. Every note gets its full duration and attention.

items.label: A piece at 60% of its target tempo (7 min)
items.instruction: Pick a piece you're learning. Play it substantially slower than performance tempo. Notice everything you notice — tension, hesitation, wrong-note habits. Don't try to fix; just observe.

items.label: Cool-down (3 min)
items.instruction: Play something you love. Slowly. End the session feeling relaxed, not stretched.
